title: Dharmaraya Swamy Temple
tagline: Dharmaraya Swamy Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Shri Dharmaraya Swamy Temple is one of the oldest and most famous Hindu temples of the city of Bengaluru, in Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Bengaluru Town Hall
topPlaces.description: Sir K. P. Puttanna Chetty Town Hall, locally commonly referred to as Bangalore Town Hall, is a neoclassical municipal building in Bangalore, India, named after the philanthropist and former president of Bangalore City Municipality, Sir K.P Puttanna Chetty.

topPlaces.name: Banappa Park
topPlaces.description: Banappa Park is a public park in Bangalore that has been used by various popular movements right from the days of Freedom Struggle or Indian independence movement. On average at least two rallies, attended by hundreds of people, are held every week at Banappa Park, off Hudson Circle.

topPlaces.name: Kaalikaamba Kamatheshwara Temple
topPlaces.description: Kaalikaamba Kamatheshwara Temple is located in Nagarathpet in Bangalore city, Karnataka, India. The city is dedicated to the deities Kaalikaamba and Kamatheshwara (the Hindu god Shiva, and goddess Shakthi or parvathi).

topPlaces.name: Hudson Memorial Church, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: The Hudson Memorial Church is located in the Bangalore Pete, Hudson Circle, surrounded by the Office of the Bangalore Corporation, Ulsoor Gate Police Station, Cubbon Park and Kanteerava Stadium. The church was established in 1904, and is a Kannada CSI church of the Bangalore Diocese.

topPlaces.name: Avenue Road, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Avenue Road (tautological name, lit. 'Road Road') is a busy shopping and commercial street in Bangalore, the state capital of Karnataka, India. It is located in Chickpet and runs through the heart of the city.

topPlaces.name: K. R. Market
topPlaces.description: K R Market (Krishnarajendra Market), also known as City Market, is the largest wholesale market dealing with commodities in Bangalore, India. It is named after Krishnarajendra Wodeyar, a former ruler of the princely state of Mysore.

topPlaces.name: Ranganathaswamy Temple, Bengaluru
topPlaces.description: Ranganathaswamy Temple, in Chikkapete, is an ancient and famous Hindu Vaikhanasa temple in Bangalore, Karnataka, India, dating back to the 16th century C.E dedicated to Lord Ranganathaswamy. The temple is built in the Vijayanagara style of architecture and has beautifully carved granite pillars reminiscent of Hoysala influence.

topPlaces.name: Rice Memorial Church
topPlaces.description: The Rice Memorial Church is located in the busy Avenue Road, Bangalore Pete. It is named after Rev.

items.note: Sir K. P. Puttanna Chetty Town Hall, locally commonly referred to as Bangalore Town Hall, is a neoclassical municipal building in Bangalore, India, named after the philanthropist and former president of Bangalore City Municipality, Sir K.P Puttanna Chetty.

Banappa Park is a public park in Bangalore that has been used by various popular movements right from the days of Freedom Struggle or Indian independence movement. On average at least two rallies, attended by hundreds of people, are held every week at Banappa Park, off Hudson Circle.

Kaalikaamba Kamatheshwara Temple is located in Nagarathpet in Bangalore city, Karnataka, India. The city is dedicated to the deities Kaalikaamba and Kamatheshwara (the Hindu god Shiva, and goddess Shakthi or parvathi).

Avenue Road (tautological name, lit. 'Road Road') is a busy shopping and commercial street in Bangalore, the state capital of Karnataka, India. It is located in Chickpet and runs through the heart of the city.

K R Market (Krishnarajendra Market), also known as City Market, is the largest wholesale market dealing with commodities in Bangalore, India. It is named after Krishnarajendra Wodeyar, a former ruler of the princely state of Mysore.

Ranganathaswamy Temple, in Chikkapete, is an ancient and famous Hindu Vaikhanasa temple in Bangalore, Karnataka, India, dating back to the 16th century C.E dedicated to Lord Ranganathaswamy. The temple is built in the Vijayanagara style of architecture and has beautifully carved granite pillars reminiscent of Hoysala influence.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dharmaraya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dharmaraya Swamy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dharmaraya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kempegowda International Airport, Bengaluru (29 km) and the nearest railway station is KSR Bengaluru City Junction (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dharmaraya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dharmaraya Swamy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dharmaraya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Dharmaraya Swamy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dharmaraya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bengaluru Town Hall, Banappa Park, Kaalikaamba Kamatheshwara Temple, Hudson Memorial Church, Bengaluru, Avenue Road, Bengaluru.
